deep_dive.title: Modularity-Free Conflict-Averse Training for Generalized PINNs
deep_dive.abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have become a powerful framework for solving PDEs by embedding physical laws into differentiable objectives. Despite their advances, training PINNs remains fragile: recent conflict-averse optimization schemes alleviate gradient interference between residual and boundary losses, but we show that their effectiveness deteriorates as model capacity increases. In this paper, we identify a capacity-induced failure mode, where overparameterized networks undergo functional modularity, self-partitioning into task-exclusive modules that suppress cross-objective interaction and hinder convergence toward Pareto-stationary points. To address this issue, we propose a novel framework, Modular-Sparsity Synchronization (ModSync), which integrates structural optimization into conflict-averse training by penalizing task-exclusive connections while preserving interaction-promoting pathways. Extensive experiments across diverse PDE benchmarks demonstrate that ModSync consistently prevents capacity-driven failures, sustains robust cross-objective coupling, and achieves state-of-the-art accuracy. Codes are available at \url{https://github.com/heejokong/ModSync}.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: PINN training and conflict avoidance for PDE solving
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于PDE求解的PINN训练及冲突规避方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于PDE求解的PINN训练及冲突规避方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于PDE求解的PINN训练及冲突规避方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Identifies capacity-induced failure in PINN training due to functional modularity, proposes ModSync to resolve gradient conflicts, improving convergence and accuracy for PDE solving.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文发现PINN在过参数化时会出现功能模块化现象，导致梯度冲突加剧。提出了模块稀疏同步（ModSync）框架，通过抑制模块化来缓解冲突，从而提升多目标PDE求解的收敛速度和精度。实验表明方法有效克服了容量增大带来的训练困难，为广义PINNs提供了更稳定的优化策略。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文发现PINN在过参数化时会出现功能模块化现象，导致梯度冲突加剧。提出了模块稀疏同步（ModSync）框架，通过抑制模块化来缓解冲突，从而提升多目标PDE求解的收敛速度和精度。实验表明方法有效克服了容量增大带来的训练困难，为广义PINNs提供了更稳定的优化策略。

quick_skim.title: Neural network surrogates with uncertainty quantification for inverse problems in partial differential equations
quick_skim.abstract: Inverse problems for differential equations arise throughout science and engineering, where one seeks to infer unknown model parameters from noisy or incomplete observations. Traditional numerical methods for these problems are often computationally expensive, particularly in Bayesian settings where evaluating the likelihood becomes costly for complex forward models and high-dimensional parameter spaces. To address this challenge, we introduce DeepGaLA, a neural-network surrogate for differential equation solvers that provides uncertainty-aware predictions, reducing overconfident inference when training data are limited. To evaluate the fidelity of the surrogate-induced posterior approximations in practice, we show that a short run of delayed-acceptance Markov chain Monte Carlo can serve as an effective diagnostic. Across a range of numerical experiments, DeepGaLA delivers forward-model approximations with accuracy comparable to established Gaussian-process surrogates, while better maintaining efficiency as parameter dimension grows. Moreover, it can incorporate differential-equation constraints, including in nonlinear settings. Overall, these results indicate that uncertainty-quantified neural surrogates can enable scalable and reliable Bayesian inference for inverse problems in complex systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: introduces DeepGaLA, a neural-network surrogate for differential equation solvers with uncertainty quantification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出DeepGaLA，一种带不确定性量化的微分方程求解器神经网络替代模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出DeepGaLA，一种带不确定性量化的微分方程求解器神经网络替代模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出DeepGaLA，一种带不确定性量化的微分方程求解器神经网络替代模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: DeepGaLA provides an uncertainty-aware neural surrogate for PDE solvers, enabling efficient Bayesian inverse problems while reducing overconfident inference under limited data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对传统PDE反问题数值方法计算成本高、贝叶斯设置下似然评估困难的问题，本文提出DeepGaLA，一种能够提供不确定性感知预测的神经网络替代模型。该模型在训练数据有限时减少过度自信推理，并通过后验近似评估其保真度。DeepGaLA为使用神经网络代理进行PDE反问题提供了可靠的不确定性量化工具，降低了物理信息机器学习中计算与统计推断的障碍。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对传统PDE反问题数值方法计算成本高、贝叶斯设置下似然评估困难的问题，本文提出DeepGaLA，一种能够提供不确定性感知预测的神经网络替代模型。该模型在训练数据有限时减少过度自信推理，并通过后验近似评估其保真度。DeepGaLA为使用神经网络代理进行PDE反问题提供了可靠的不确定性量化工具，降低了物理信息机器学习中计算与统计推断的障碍。

quick_skim.title: Assessing Reliability of Symbol Detection in Concept Bottleneck Models
quick_skim.abstract: Concept Bottleneck Models (CBMs) are a relevant tool for explainable Artificial Intelligence because they make their predictions through human-interpretable symbols. However, high task accuracy does not guarantee that these symbols are detected faithfully: jointly trained CBMs may encode task-specific shortcuts in the bottleneck, making their explanations unreliable. In this paper, we study concept-detection reliability by swapping independently trained concept detectors and classification heads that share the same symbolic vocabulary. We use the resulting performance degradation, concept-level metrics, and symbol-wise uncertainty estimates to identify concepts that are especially prone to spurious firing. Finally, we propose a reliability-aware training strategy in which a shared concept detector is optimized with multiple classification heads and penalized for relying on globally or instance-wise unreliable symbols. On CUB-200-2011 with full concept supervision, detectors and heads are almost freely interchangeable (swap drop below one accuracy point, relative retention above $99\%$, and no concept detected below chance), whereas on a controlled synthetic task we show that, as the concept-supervision weight is reduced, models keep near-perfect task accuracy while swapped accuracy and agreement with the ground-truth concepts collapse to chance. Our reliability-aware training substantially mitigates this leakage, roughly doubling swap accuracy in the leaky regime.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reliability of symbol detection in concept bottleneck models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 概念瓶颈模型中符号检测的可靠性
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 概念瓶颈模型中符号检测的可靠性
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 概念瓶颈模型中符号检测的可靠性
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Assesses symbol detection reliability in CBMs by swapping independent detectors and heads, identifying spurious concepts and proposing reliability-aware training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 概念瓶颈模型（CBM）依赖符号进行预测，但联合训练可能导致符号检测不忠实。本文通过交换独立训练的概念检测器和分类头评估可靠性，发现部分概念易出现虚假触发。进而提出可靠性感知训练策略，提升符号检测的忠实度，增强模型可解释性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 概念瓶颈模型（CBM）依赖符号进行预测，但联合训练可能导致符号检测不忠实。本文通过交换独立训练的概念检测器和分类头评估可靠性，发现部分概念易出现虚假触发。进而提出可靠性感知训练策略，提升符号检测的忠实度，增强模型可解释性。
